heading: Doc Brown
raw: If my calculations are correct, when this baby hits 88 miles per hour, you're gonna see some serious shit.
clean: If my calculations are correct, when this baby hits 88 miles per hour, you're gonna see some serious shit.

heading: Doc Brown
raw: Next Saturday night, we're sending you back to the future!
clean: Next Saturday night, we're sending you back to the future!

heading: Doc Brown
raw: I'm sure that in 1985, plutonium is available in every corner drugstore, but in 1955, it's a little hard to come by.
clean: I'm sure that in 1985, plutonium is available in every corner drugstore, but in 1955, it's a little hard to come by.

heading: Doc Brown
raw: Great Scott!
clean: Great Scott!

heading: Biff Tannen
raw: Since you're new here, I-I'm gonna cut you a break, today. So, why don't you make like a tree and get outta here?
clean: Since you're new here, I-I'm gonna cut you a break, today. So, why don't you make like a tree and get outta here?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: [to Jennifer] You know, this time it wasn't my fault. The Doc set all his clocks 25 minutes slow.
clean: You know, this time it wasn't my fault. The Doc set all his clocks 25 minutes slow.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: [sarcastically] Oh, yes, sir.
clean: Oh, yes, sir.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Strickland: You got a real attitude problem, McFly. You're a slacker. You remind me of your father when he went here. He was a slacker, too.
clean: You got a real attitude problem, McFly. You're a slacker. You remind me of your father when he went here. He was a slacker, too.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Can I go now, Mr. Strickland?
clean: Can I go now, Mr. Strickland?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Strickland: I noticed your band is on the roster for the dance auditions after school today. Why even bother, McFly? You don't have a chance. You're too much like your old man. No McFly ever amounted to anything in the history of Hill Valley!
clean: I noticed your band is on the roster for the dance auditions after school today. Why even bother, McFly? You don't have a chance. You're too much like your old man. No McFly ever amounted to anything in the history of Hill Valley!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Yeah, well, history is gonna change.
clean: Yeah, well, history is gonna change.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: "Too loud". I can't believe it. I'm never gonna get a chance to play in front of anybody.
clean: "Too loud". I can't believe it. I'm never gonna get a chance to play in front of anybody.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jennifer: Marty, one rejection isn't the end of the world.
clean: Marty, one rejection isn't the end of the world.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Nah, I just don't think I'm cut out for music.
clean: Nah, I just don't think I'm cut out for music.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jennifer: But you're good, Marty, you're really good. And this audition tape of yours is great, you gotta send it in to the record company. It's like Doc's always saying.
clean: But you're good, Marty, you're really good. And this audition tape of yours is great, you gotta send it in to the record company. It's like Doc's always saying.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Yeah. I know, I know. "If you put your mind to it, you could accomplish anything."
clean: Yeah. I know, I know. "If you put your mind to it, you could accomplish anything."

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jennifer: That's good advice, Marty.
clean: That's good advice, Marty.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Alright, okay, Jennifer. What if I send in the tape and they don't like it? I mean, what if they say I'm no good? What if they say, "Get outta here, kid, you got no future." I mean, I just don't think I can take that kind of rejection. (sighs) Jesus, I'm beginning to sound like my old man.
clean: Alright, okay, Jennifer. What if I send in the tape and they don't like it? I mean, what if they say I'm no good? What if they say, "Get outta here, kid, you got no future." I mean, I just don't think I can take that kind of rejection. (sighs) Jesus, I'm beginning to sound like my old man.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lorraine: [about Jennifer] I don't like her, Marty. Any girl who just calls up a boy is just asking for trouble.
clean: I don't like her, Marty. Any girl who just calls up a boy is just asking for trouble.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Linda: Oh, Mom, there's nothing wrong with calling a boy.
clean: Oh, Mom, there's nothing wrong with calling a boy.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lorraine: I think it's terrible; Girls chasing boys. When I was your age, I never chased a boy or called a boy or sat in a parked car with a boy.
clean: I think it's terrible; Girls chasing boys. When I was your age, I never chased a boy or called a boy or sat in a parked car with a boy.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Linda: Then how am I supposed to ever meet anybody?
clean: Then how am I supposed to ever meet anybody?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lorraine: Well, it will just happen, like the way I met your father.
clean: Well, it will just happen, like the way I met your father.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Linda: That was so stupid! Grandpa hit him with the car.
clean: That was so stupid! Grandpa hit him with the car.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lorraine: [wistfully] It was meant to be. Anyway, if Grandpa hadn't hit him, then none of you would have been born.
clean: It was meant to be. Anyway, if Grandpa hadn't hit him, then none of you would have been born.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Linda: Yeah, well, I still don't understand what Dad was doing in the middle of the street.
clean: Yeah, well, I still don't understand what Dad was doing in the middle of the street.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lorraine: What was it, George? Birdwatching?
clean: What was it, George? Birdwatching?

heading: Dialogue
raw: George: [distracted, not really listening] What, Lorraine? What?
clean: What, Lorraine? What?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lorraine: Anyway, your grandpa hit him with the car and brought him into the house. He seemed so helpless, like a little lost puppy. And my heart just went out to him.
clean: Anyway, your grandpa hit him with the car and brought him into the house. He seemed so helpless, like a little lost puppy. And my heart just went out to him.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Linda: Yeah, Mom, we know. You've told us this story a million times. You felt sorry for him, so you decided to go with him to the Fish Under the Sea dance.
clean: Yeah, Mom, we know. You've told us this story a million times. You felt sorry for him, so you decided to go with him to the Fish Under the Sea dance.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: Ha! What did I tell you?! 88 MILES PER HOUR! The temporal displacement occurred exactly 1:20 A.M. and zero seconds!
clean: Ha! What did I tell you?! 88 MILES PER HOUR! The temporal displacement occurred exactly 1:20 A.M. and zero seconds!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Ah, Jesus Christ! Jesus Christ, Doc, you disintegrated Einstein!
clean: Ah, Jesus Christ! Jesus Christ, Doc, you disintegrated Einstein!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: Calm down, Marty! [takes down notes] I didn't disintegrate anything! The molecular structure of both Einstein and the car are completely intact.
clean: Calm down, Marty! I didn't disintegrate anything! The molecular structure of both Einstein and the car are completely intact.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Then, where the hell are they?!
clean: Then, where the hell are they?!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: The appropriate question is, "When the hell are they?"! You see, Einstein has just become the world's first time-traveler! I sent him into the future. One minute into the future, to be exact. And at precisely 1:21 A.M. and zero seconds, we shall catch up with him and the time machine!
clean: The appropriate question is, "When the hell are they?"! You see, Einstein has just become the world's first time-traveler! I sent him into the future. One minute into the future, to be exact. And at precisely 1:21 A.M. and zero seconds, we shall catch up with him and the time machine!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Wait a minute. Wait a minute Doc, uh, are you telling me you built a time machine... out of a DeLorean?
clean: Wait a minute. Wait a minute Doc, uh, are you telling me you built a time machine... out of a DeLorean?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: The way I see it, if you're gonna build a time machine into a car, why not do it with some style? Besides, the stainless-steel construction made the flux dispersal-- [his watch beeps] Look out! [the Delorean reappears]
clean: The way I see it, if you're gonna build a time machine into a car, why not do it with some style? Besides, the stainless-steel construction made the flux dispersal-- Look out!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lou: Are you gonna order somethin’, kid?
clean: Are you gonna order somethin', kid?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Yeah, gimme a Tab.
clean: Yeah, gimme a Tab.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lou: Tab? I can't give you a tab unless you order somethin'.
clean: Tab? I can't give you a tab unless you order somethin'.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Right, gimme a Pepsi Free.
clean: Right, gimme a Pepsi Free.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lou: You wanna a Pepsi, pal, you're gonna pay for it.
clean: You wanna a Pepsi, pal, you're gonna pay for it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Well just gimme somethin’ without any sugar in it, okay?
clean: Well just gimme somethin' without any sugar in it, okay?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lou: Without any sugar.
clean: Without any sugar.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: [sees Marty's picture, still thinking Marty is lying to him] Pretty mediocre photographic fakery. They cut off your brother's hair.
clean: Pretty mediocre photographic fakery. They cut off your brother's hair.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: I'm tellin' the truth, Doc. You gotta believe me.
clean: I'm tellin' the truth, Doc. You gotta believe me.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: Then tell me, Future Boy, who's President of the United States in 1985?
clean: Then tell me, Future Boy, who's President of the United States in 1985?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Ronald Reagan.
clean: Ronald Reagan.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: Ronald Reagan? The actor? [scoffs] Then who's vice-president? Jerry Lewis? I suppose Jane Wyman is the First Lady?
clean: Ronald Reagan? The actor? Then who's vice-president? Jerry Lewis? I suppose Jane Wyman is the First Lady?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Whoa, wait. Doc!
clean: Whoa, wait. Doc!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: And Jack Benny is Secretary of the Treasury!
clean: And Jack Benny is Secretary of the Treasury!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Doc, you gotta listen to me!
clean: Doc, you gotta listen to me!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: I've had enough practical jokes for one evening! Good night, future boy! [slams door]
clean: I've had enough practical jokes for one evening! Good night, future boy!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: This is more serious than I thought. Apparently, your mother is amorously infatuated with you instead of your father.
clean: This is more serious than I thought. Apparently, your mother is amorously infatuated with you instead of your father.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Whoa, wait a minute, Doc. Are you tryin' to tell me that my mother...has got the hots for me?
clean: Whoa, wait a minute, Doc. Are you tryin' to tell me that my mother...has got the hots for me?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: Precisely!
clean: Precisely!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Whoa, this is heavy.
clean: Whoa, this is heavy.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: There's that word again: "heavy." Why are things so heavy in the future? Is there a problem with the Earth's gravitational pull?
clean: There's that word again: "heavy." Why are things so heavy in the future? Is there a problem with the Earth's gravitational pull?

heading: Dialogue
raw: George: Lorraine. My density has bought me to you.
clean: Lorraine. My density has bought me to you.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lorraine: [confused] What?
clean: What?

heading: Dialogue
raw: George: [consults his notepad] Oh. What I meant to say was...
clean: Oh. What I meant to say was...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lorraine: Wait a minute. Don't I know you from somewhere?
clean: Wait a minute. Don't I know you from somewhere?

heading: Dialogue
raw: George: Yes. Yes. I'm George. George McFly. I'm your density. I mean, your destiny.
clean: Yes. Yes. I'm George. George McFly. I'm your density. I mean, your destiny.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lorraine: Marty, why are you so nervous?
clean: Marty, why are you so nervous?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Lorraine, have you ever, uh, been in a situation where you knew you had to act a certain way, but when you got there, you didn't know if you could go through with it?
clean: Lorraine, have you ever, uh, been in a situation where you knew you had to act a certain way, but when you got there, you didn't know if you could go through with it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lorraine: You mean, like how you're supposed to act on a first date?
clean: You mean, like how you're supposed to act on a first date?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Well, sort of.
clean: Well, sort of.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lorraine: Oh, I, I think I know exactly what you mean.
clean: Oh, I, I think I know exactly what you mean.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Y-y-you do?
clean: Y-y-you do?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lorraine: You know what I do in those situations?
clean: You know what I do in those situations?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: What?
clean: What?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Lorraine: I don't worry. [kisses him hard, then stops and pulls back to see Marty is shocked] This is all wrong. I don't know what it is, but when I kiss you, it's like I'm kissing... my brother. I guess that doesn't make any sense, does it?
clean: I don't worry. This is all wrong. I don't know what it is, but when I kiss you, it's like I'm kissing... my brother. I guess that doesn't make any sense, does it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Believe me, it makes perfect sense.
clean: Believe me, it makes perfect sense.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: [surprised] You're alive! [Doc unbuttons his suit to reveal the bulletproof vest] Bulletproof vest? How did you know? I di-I never got a chance to tell you. [Doc reveals Marty's torn-up 1955 letter which has been taped back together] What about all that talk about screwing up future events? The spacetime continuum?
clean: You're alive! Bulletproof vest? How did you know? I di-I never got a chance to tell you. What about all that talk about screwing up future events? The spacetime continuum?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: Well, I figured, "what the hell?"
clean: Well, I figured, "what the hell?"

heading: Dialogue
raw: Marty: Hey, Doc, (I think) we better back up. We don't have enough road to get up to 88.
clean: Hey, Doc, (I think) we better back up. We don't have enough road to get up to 88.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Doc: Roads? Where we're going, we don't need roads.
clean: Roads? Where we're going, we don't need roads.
